summary: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast for body text, 3:1 for ≥18px bold headlines; white on #92278F/#262262/#01A47E pass; avoid silver or periwinkle on white.
All images require alt text; email must convey indication and safety without images; ISI content must be live text, not image-based.
Accessibility: live text ≥10px footnotes, body text ≥12px, tap targets ≥44px, logical heading order with single H1 per email.
Use exactly one H1 per email with logical heading order for accessibility.
Underline all links; avoid using color alone as the link indicator for accessibility.
Use semantic <table role="presentation"> markup for all layout tables to improve accessibility.
Content sections use self-contained 600px tables with individual backgrounds, top/bottom padding, optional wave edges, no outer margins, stacking flush with Top/End Matter.
Sections must declare theme backgrounds at top/bottom edges for seamless or wave transitions; first section meets header on white/theme; last ends with footer-approved wave background.
Footnote symbols (* † ‡ §) run continuously email-wide, not per section; references numbered across sections, placed above/within locked End Matter per template.
One Wave graphic and one key-strategic-question callout per email; one primary CTA per section; max ~3 CTAs per email.
No section may introduce fonts, colors, button shapes, or icon styles outside this document; new icons must match existing line weight and simplified line style.
Content sections must not reproduce logo lockups or ISI/legal copy; only Top/End Matter sections may contain these locked components.
Final assembly QA: body size = locked ISI body size (12 px/1.4), audience per header/footer (§4.2), all §1.8 content present, consistent theme, no horizontal scroll at both breakpoints.
Use branded palette with 30/30/20/15/5 ratio; Purple (#92278F), Dark Blue (#262262), White (#FFFFFF) are primary, emphasizing Purple; Silver, Dark Gray, Green as accents only.
Do not use Purple or Green for body copy; use Green (tok_ibsrela_color_green) only for CTA fills and accents; Dark Gray for charts/UI; Silver never for text.
Text colors: Titles #92278F Purple; Headlines & subheads #262262 Dark Blue; Body & ISI #000000 Black; correct typo '#00000' to #000000.
Title and first email line always purple; no other email text may be purple.
In white sections, body text highlights must be black and bold; blue or purple are prohibited in paragraph body text.
No mixing colors within a sentence; each sentence (heading or body) must use a single color only.
Flip The Script campaign uses Periwinkle #7B7BED, Purple #92278F, Green #01A47E evenly (33% each) as accents, not replacing the branded palette.
Scene-banner fill colors must match fixed pairs: Periwinkle-Office, Purple-Cafe, Green-Soccer; no interchange allowed in content_tag variants.
Callout opacity over white: Periwinkle 80%, Purple 60%, Green 40%; white banner over imagery 80% opacity.
Primary gradient: #262262@0% → midpoint@40% → #92278F@100%, secondary light gradient: #CFDCE3 → #FFFFFF, ideal angle 45°, adjustable rotation allowed.
Dark Blue → Purple gradient applies sparingly to headlines/callouts, e.g., two-tone headline with back half in Purple.
Use gradients only as email section background images/CSS with #92278F/#262262 fallback; never gradient body text—use two-color split headline (navy + purple) instead.
Apply exactly one theme per email; theme variables style frame and automatically restyle tile, button, and campaign-image colors; no independent section theming.

Email themes limited to White, Silver/light, Purple (#92278F), Navy (#262262), Green (#01A47E); light themes use dark text and teal/green buttons; dark themes use white text, knockout logo, light/outlined buttons.
Apply white text and underlined white links on Purple/Navy/Green panels; navy headlines, black body, purple accents, and purple/navy underlined links on White/Silver panels.
DTP emails alternate backgrounds: purple hero → white content (efficacy + side-effects) → white/light photo (story/lifestyle) → green #01A47E accent panel → purple campaign image → locked End Matter.
Sections have no edge margin; transitions use background color changes or full-bleed photographic heroes, never dividers or rules.
Green panel (#01A47E) appears once per email, reserved for either affordability block or symptom-icon trio, never both stacked.

On LIGHT backgrounds (White, Silver) use green-filled CTA button with white label; on DARK backgrounds (Dark Gray, Purple, Dark Blue, Green, Teal, Periwinkle) use white-filled button with green label.
CTA section text colors follow background group: light background headings Dark Blue/body Black; dark background heading/body White; image CTAs (#92278F, #0F99BC, #7B7BED) always white text.

In CTA section, use one image from three locked sets: Email_Primary_2_column.png on Purple #92278F (default), Email_Primary_column_golf.png on Teal #0F99BC (LPGA), or Cake_lockup.png on Periwinkle #7B7BED (alt); swap image and background color simultaneously, no cross-usage.
LPGA-gated: golf asset ast_ibsrela_cta_email_primary_column_golf and Teal #0F99BC background only used with LPGA content; teal reserved for this CTA section only.
If content section is directly above CTA, CTA runs image-free with text + button on approved background; button uses light/dark flip per background rules.
All components must appear in both desktop (600px width) and mobile (375px width) layouts; SFMC auto-serves the correct version.
Use 600px fixed-width tables for emails; apply fluid/hybrid technique to collapse to 375px design width and 100% fluid below 480px breakpoint (email.fluid_breakpoint).
Component reference heights (Primary 1-Col 600×1138/375×788; Primary 2-Col 600×1088/375×1538; Secondary 600×458 desktop) are proportional, not fixed.
Spacing values must be multiples of 8 px; adjust layouts only in 8 px increments, avoiding any odd or one-off spacing values.
Outer padding 24px desktop/16px mobile; section vertical 32px desktop/24px mobile; two-column gutter 24px desktop/24px stacked mobile; card/internal padding 16px all; image-text gap 16px.
Adjacent sections use only padding, no margin between stacked tables; sections must be edge-to-edge for flush backgrounds and wave shapes.
Use a 12-column grid on 552 px content (600 − 2×24 px padding); half column equals 264 px content + 24 px gutter.
callout.key_question.border: rounded rectangle, white outline, transparent fill, used only for key strategic questions like 'DO YOU WANT TO TRY SOMETHING DIFFERENT?'
Use white banner callout at 80% opacity over background for 'IBSRELA WORKS DIFFERENTLY…' claim.
Scene-paired banner callouts: Office=Periwinkle 80%, Cafe=Purple 60%, Soccer=Green 40% opacity on white; no mixing scene-color pairs.
Use the approved icon trio for IBS-C symptoms left-to-right: Constipation, Belly Pain, Bloating; circular green-tinted icons with navy #262262 labels beneath.
All three symptom icons must appear together in the specified order with no reordering, isolation, or alternate style substitutions.
Symptom-icon trio on green/teal panel or white above it, followed by headline, supporting copy, and CTA.
All text and CTA buttons are left-aligned; CTA buttons align flush with the left edge of the text block above, never centered or right-aligned.
In CTA section, supporting images appear right of text in two-column layout; without images, text is left-aligned and spans full column width.

Use Avenir hierarchy: H1 - Black, Title Case, #262262/#92278F; H2 - Black, sentence case, #262262/#92278F; Body/ISI body - Roman, sentence case, #000000; ISI subheads - Black, ALL CAPS; Footnotes - Book, sentence case, black; Campaign headlines - Black, ALL CAPS, campaign palette; Callouts/CTAs - Black, ALL CAPS, per button spec.
ISI boxed warning text uses exclusively Avenir Heavy, color Black #000000, case per approved ISI.
Use Arial for Avenir in email/PowerPoint: Avenir Black/Heavy → Arial Bold; Avenir Roman/Book → Arial Regular; email font stack 'Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif'; no Avenir webfonts in email.
Typography: H1 main section headline 24px desktop/22px mobile Bold line-height 1.25; H2 16px Bold 1.5; body 16px Regular 1.5; CTA 14px Bold 1.2 white pill teal #01A47E label.

End Matter ISI/legal footer uses locked scale: ISI subheads 14px Bold, 'IMPORTANT RISK INFORMATION' 15px Bold, ISI body 13px Regular (1.4 lh), footnotes/legal 11px Regular; not styled by editorial defaults.
End Matter—ISI texts and legal footer/unsubscribe block fixed system constants; never restyled or altered by content defaults.
Body text letter-spacing is 0; ALL-CAPS callouts and CTAs use +0.5px; negative tracking prohibited in email.
Paragraph spacing in sections: headline→subhead 8px; subhead→body 12px; headline→body (no subhead) 12px; body paragraphs 12px; body→CTA button 16px.
Spacing owned by section halves gaps; Editorial white sections 12x24x12x24 px padding; Dark feature 24px all sides; Inset card radius 12px, 24px padding; side gutters always 24px.
Padding tokens fixed: Editorial→Editorial 24px; Editorial↔Dark feature 36px; Dark feature→Dark feature 48px; full-bleed hero/swirl→section 12px.
Never override vertical padding tokens; no empty spacer rows or placeholder cells as they add 12–16px and disrupt spacing calculations.
Headlines use Title Case (h1.case=title_case), body copy sentence case (body.case=sentence_case), callouts and CTAs all caps (callout.case=all_caps, cta.button.label.case=all_caps).
Campaign-specific headlines (FLIP THE SCRIPT) use ALL CAPS, overriding default Title Case headline casing.
campaign.body.case=title_case for campaign names in body copy; capitalize each word like 'Flip The Script', not all caps or lowercase.
'FLIP THE SCRIPT' lockup flips 'THE' upside down; 'ON IBS-C' uses Avenir Light, 50% opacity with hyphen replaced by 100% opacity brand swoosh (§2.6).
Bold Arial Bold is used for headlines, subheads, card titles, CTA labels, ISI subheads, boxed-warning text, and MLR-approved key claim phrases only.
Do not bold entire body paragraphs, footnotes, references, or underlined links; limit consecutive bold words in body copy to ~10.
Avoid bold+italic in email body; use Arial Italic only for journal titles in references or as per approved copy deck.
Never use bold for hierarchy; promote prominent text to subhead or approved callout style instead.
SFMC emails stack: (1) Top Matter LOCKED, (2) optional Primary 1 Col, (3) optional Primary 2 Col, (4) optional repeatable Secondary, (5) End Matter LOCKED.
Top Matter and End Matter are LOCKED, supplied externally, and outside section-designer scope; do not build or modify them.
Select one of the two wireframe templates, remove unneeded sections, do not add new ones; adjust content to fit, only omit existing elements, no new element types allowed.
Provide Desktop (600px) and Mobile (375px) layouts for all components; SFMC auto-serves device-appropriate version; QA both breakpoints for every content section.
First content section flush against Top Matter bottom edge (white/theme background, optional top wave-edge); last content section flush against End Matter with wave-edge ISI panel below.

Top Matter is pre-built, locked per audience (DTP: logo sans BID + patient safety; HCP: logo with BID), includes logo lockup, mandatory safety message with Med Guide/full PI links, View in Browser link; no IBSRELA logo repeats inside content, only in header or approved imagery.
Email content sections and imagery must align with the audience specified in the supplied header.
End Matter pre-built, LOCKED; includes full FDA-reviewed ISI, Ardelyx contact, copyright/trademark/job code, unsubscribe links; no ISI/legal duplication in content sections.
Content-section body copy must match ISI body settings: 12 px size and 1.4 line spacing as per locked footer (§1.2).
References and footnotes in content sections resolve in/above End Matter; numbering and symbol order coordinated at assembly (§4.5).

Email Primary 1 Column layout: single column (~600x1138 desktop, ~375x788 mobile) with full-width hero image, purple H1, bold subheadline, body copy, teal 'Get connected' CTA; background color theme, optional top wave borders; editable hero, headlines, copy, CTA label; ideal for lead message with single visual and clear call to action.
Email Primary 2 Column layout uses ~600×1088 desktop or ~375×1538 stacking mobile, brand purple gradient background, white text, outlined CTA; text left, image right.

Email Secondary layout: two-column ~600×458 desktop, ~375×1363 mobile stacked; cards with brand image, navy headline, body copy, teal CTA; toggles for columns, image position, subhead, button; editable content.
Patient-facing DTP copy uses second person ("you", "your") and frames IBSRELA as aiding return to everyday life, not clinical performance.
Lead with empathetic acknowledgment of unresolved symptoms (e.g., "If you're still experiencing… you don't have to settle for feeling just okay") before product benefit.
Use "IBS-C symptoms" (constipation, belly pain, bloating), avoid clinical jargon, consistently list all three symptoms together when stating benefits.
Patient-story modules require disclosure under story copy: "Individual results may vary. [Name] is an actual compensated IBSRELA patient discussing her experience.

Efficacy claims require qualifier "*Results may vary. Study results seen in 12- and 26-week trials…" plus limitation "These results were observed in the study, but the analysis cannot be used to draw conclusions.
DTP CTA labels: 1–3 word short verb phrases in sentence case (not ALL CAPS), e.g. "Explore IBSRELA", "Watch video", override all-caps default.
Rule (cta): Use a single solid-fill primary CTA style for poll/segmentation question response buttons (e.g., "Yes, I have").
DTP CTAs must avoid HCP default "Get connected" and use section-specific labels like patient story "Watch [name]'s story" or product overview "Explore IBSRELA".
